text: A medium-sized tampon mounted on a pair of forceps and inserted through the utility incision was used to retract the pericardium and the heart to better expose the retroscleronal space.
CO2 induced pneumothorax was not used.
The larger, more caudal lymph node was dissected extrapleurally without the need for mammary vessel dissection and then resected with minor bleeding due to extrapleural isolation.
After careful dissection of the retrosternal space from the preparicardial fat, the smaller more cranial lymph node was dissected in a similar fashion and resected spurring the mammary vessels.
Haemostasis was then optimized by fibrin goes and coagulation.
